chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 1144 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.6821/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on MA No.6822/2025 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of bail application 25.11.2025 Mr. Aqleem Hyder Shar, Advocate along with applicants / accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is allowed. 2. Learned Counsel undertakes to comply to office objection No. (i) by way of correction in the record. Let the same be corrected in the office, and the other objection be complied with within three days. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4. Applicants / accused Qamaruddin S/o Gulab, Nizamuddin S/o Gulab, Saeed S/o Jalaluddin, Jamshed S/o Jalaluddin, Gulab Khan alias Aamil S/o Muhakamuddin, Yasir S/o Muhakamuddin, Kamil S/o Muhakamuddin, Muhammad Ramzan S/o Muhammad Chattal, Inzmam S/o Nizamuddin (as per affidavit), Waheed Ali S/o Kaloo, Yar Muhammad S/o Mehboob and Tarique Hussain S/o Bagan seek protective bail in Crime No.174/2025, registered at Police Station Kotdiji, District Khairpur, under Sections 384, 397, 337-H, 506/2, PPC. It is, inter alia, contended that applicants / accused are prepared to surrender before the trial Court, but apprehend their arrest as NBWs have been issued against them. Without touching merits of the case, protective bail is granted to the applicants / accused for a period of five days, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- (Rupees thirty thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court.

It is, inter alia, contended that applicants / accused are prepared to surrender before the trial Court, but apprehend their arrest as NBWs have been issued against them. Without touching merits of the case, protective bail is granted to the applicants / accused for a period of five days, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.30,000/- (Rupees thirty thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of the NBWs shall stand suspended for the period of five days. Applicants / accused shall surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have any effect after expiry of five days or when the applicants / accused surrender before the trial Court, whichever occurs earlier. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
